plans.title: Protector.Net Action Plans
plans.description: Select which trigger plans to clone (as System) and expose as Action Plan buttons.

data.plans: Select Action Plans to Import (If There Is Any)

entity_selection.title: Door Entities (Optional)
entity_selection.description: ⚠️ These legacy button entities are optional and only needed for backwards compatibility. You can leave this empty — the new Override switch/selects provide the same functionality. Pulse Unlock is always added automatically.

data.entities: Select What Door Entities to Enable (Leave Empty If Not Needed)

reconfigure.title: Update Protector.Net Credentials
reconfigure.description: Update the credentials or URL for **{name}** without removing the integration. All entities, options, and HA-managed door schedules are preserved.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate Protector.Net
reauth_confirm.description: The Protector.Net server rejected the stored credentials for **{name}**. Please re-enter the username and password.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect. Check the URL and that the server is reachable.
error.invalid_auth: The server rejected the username or password.

abort.already_configured: Another configured entry is already using that URL and partition.

basic_settings.title: Basic Settings
basic_settings.description: Adjust Action Plan buttons, override duration, and temp code settings. Pulse Unlock is always included automatically.

data.plan_ids: Select Action Plans to Enable (If There Is Any)

legacy_entities_section.name: Legacy Door Buttons (optional)
legacy_entities_section.description: ⚠️ These legacy button entities are optional and only needed for backwards compatibility. You can leave this empty — the new Override switch/selects provide the same functionality. Pulse Unlock is always added automatically.

data.auto_add_new_doors: Auto-add new doors found in Hartmann

data_description.auto_add_new_doors: When ticked, new doors that appear in Hartmann after this point will be automatically managed AND activated by HA on the next hourly sync. They get a 24/7 'CardOrPin' Door Time Zone created and start following the HA schedule immediately. Existing doors are never touched by this. Leave off if you want to opt in each new door manually.

managed_doors_section.name: Create Door Time Zones (HA-controlled schedules)
managed_doors_section.description:
Doors that HA will create a dedicated Door Time Zone for in Hartmann (named like 'HA[abc1234] Front Door' — an HA prefix with a short id, plus the door's name).

Creating the Door Time Zone alone does NOT change door behavior — the door keeps following its existing Hartmann schedule until you also tick it under 'Activate Door Time Zones' below.

Nothing on this page does anything until you tick the 'Apply changes' checkbox below — opening this page is safe.

Unticking a door later (with Apply ticked) rolls its schedule back to original and deletes the HA Door Time Zone.

data.apply_managed_changes: Apply changes to managed doors on submit

data_description.apply_managed_changes: Required to make any changes here. If unticked, the door list below is ignored on submit and your existing managed doors stay as they are.

active_doors_section.name: Activate Door Time Zones (switch to HA schedules now)
active_doors_section.description:
Subset of the doors above whose schedule HA actually controls right now. Ticking a door here flips its DoorTimeZoneId to the HA Door Time Zone we created above and pushes the change to panels.

From there, use the 'set_door_schedule_mode' service to set the door's access mode (Lockdown, Card, Pin, CardOrPin, Unlock, etc.) — the service rewrites the HA Door Time Zone for the whole week and the panels pick up the change automatically.

Nothing on this page does anything until you tick the 'Apply changes' checkbox below — opening this page is safe.

Unticking a door later (with Apply ticked) rolls the door back to its original schedule without removing the HA Door Time Zone.

data.apply_active_changes: Apply changes to active doors on submit

data_description.apply_active_changes: Required to make any changes here. If unticked, the door list below is ignored on submit and your existing active doors stay as they are.

create_temp_code.name: Create Temp Code
create_temp_code.description: Create a temporary PIN code for door access

door_device_id.name: Door(s)
door_device_id.description: The door(s) to create the code for

code_name.name: Code Name
code_name.description: A friendly name for this code (e.g., 'Guest Room 101')

code_digits.name: Code Digits
code_digits.description: Number of digits for the random code (4-9)

start_time.name: Start Time
start_time.description: When the code becomes active (ISO datetime)

delete_temp_code.name: Delete Temp Code
delete_temp_code.description: Delete a temporary PIN code from door(s)

door_device_id.name: Door(s)
door_device_id.description: The door(s) to delete the code from

delete_temp_code_by_name.name: Delete Temp Code by Name
delete_temp_code_by_name.description: Delete a temporary PIN code by its name (for calendar-based automations)

door_device_id.name: Door(s)
door_device_id.description: The door(s) to delete the code from

code_name.name: Code Name
code_name.description: The name of the code to delete (must match exactly)

override_door.name: Override Door
override_door.description: Apply an override to door(s) (unlock, lockdown, etc.)

override_type.name: Override Type
override_type.description: How long the override should last

set_door_schedule_mode.name: Set Door Schedule Mode (HA-managed)
set_door_schedule_mode.description: Change the schedule mode for HA-managed doors. Persists across panel reboots.

door_device_id.name: Door(s)
door_device_id.description: The door(s) to update. Must be HA-managed (added under integration options).

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: Schedule mode for the entire week, 24/7.
